reviews.review_msg: I have had her for both criminology 213 and criminology 101.  She's an amazing prof, extremely organized, nice, approachable and clear.  It's not that the material itself is easy (it's university), her teaching style just makes it easy!

Camilla is a great teacher. she is super cute and tries hard to ensure that everyone gets the material.the exams are totally easy(our class average was 80%);i only used the text wen she made references to certain pages that were important;TA Annmarie is great- very helpful.the assignment n paper were easy (mostly opinion based).

Camilla is a very friendly professor. Lecture materials are fun and interesting. This course is good because it encourages you to think about the issues rather than memorizing. The assignments are all all right.  This is one of the best CRIM classes I took at SFU.
awsome teacher, EXTREMELY nice..exams were really easy. Assignments were not that easy but they were do-able.. Overall, great class and and even better teacher!
Good teacher and really passionate about what she is teaching.  Midterm wasn't too bad, and the assignment was pretty interesting! Would recommend this class!
So far...I've always gotten A- ~ A+ papers... She gave me C.****. She is a nice person though. But really hard marker. Should I get it reevaluated or something?
Her exams are fair and easy. However, she is such a hard marker on term-paper.

Sarah is an amazing prof, probably one of my favorites at sfu. She makes the class content interesting and you can tell she genuinely cares about it. She was always open to questions and even let us make up a question/answer key for the final. Great prof would deff recommend.
this was for CRIM 304 - she was so amazing but her grading scale is intense. no final exam tho which is amazing!
